data.narrative:
Firefly Aerospace, a publicly traded Space & Aerospace company, carries a current valuation of $6.3B following its $868M IPO. The peer universe comprises 3 same-sector, same-stage public comparables: Rocket Lab ($37.0B), Planet Labs ($7.0B), and Astroscale ($900M), alongside 8 private-stage peers including SpaceX and Blue Origin.

The peer-median method is applicable and yields a $7.0B implied valuation. This approach anchors on the median across the 3 public peers, establishing a reference point against Firefly's current $6.3B mark. The peer range spans $900M to $37.0B, reflecting wide dispersion in the Space & Aerospace public market. Revenue-based triangulation via public-comp P/S multexing cannot be applied; Firefly's revenue is not disclosed, preventing application of the sector median P/S of 45.3x across 6 tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

The analysis supports a valuation range of $6.0B to $8.0B, centered on the peer median of $7.0B. Firefly's current $6.3B valuation sits at the lower bound of this range, representing a 10% discount to peer median. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion among public comps—a 41x gap between Astroscale and Rocket Lab—and the absence of revenue-based valuation anchors. Stage maturity and near-term revenue trajectory remain material drivers of relative positioning within the indicated range.
